title: sertula arvensis
intro: Sertula arvensis, also known as corn marigold or corn chamomile, is a flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Sertula arvensis include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and promote skin healing. Sertula arvensis is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
